prd.oneLiner: A workplace asset-management system that connects cataloging, checkout and return, inventory audits, maintenance context, and operating reports.
prd.goal: Replace manual asset ledgers and reduce the time required for regular inventory audits by 50% within three months of launch.
prd.whyNow: More laptops, monitors, and shared equipment are being tracked across spreadsheets and chat, causing lost assets, unclear ownership, and duplicate purchases.
prd.problem: Operators cannot immediately tell where an asset is or who uses it, while employees cannot easily check availability or their return date.
prd.solution: Provide one source of truth for asset registration and QR identity, status, location and ownership, checkout and return, inventory audits, and operations reporting.
prd.alternatives: Spreadsheets, paper checkout ledgers, chat requests, and manual inventory counts.
prd.differentiator: Connect the asset list to its real checkout lifecycle, audit outcome, and maintenance context so operations decisions have evidence.

targets.name: Asset operator
targets.needs: Accurately manage asset location, condition, assignee, and checkout history.
targets.pain: Inventory counts and overdue follow-up are manual, and asset status is unreliable.

targets.name: Employee
targets.needs: Quickly find, request, and return the equipment they need.
targets.pain: They must ask an operator whether equipment is available and when it is due back.

targets.name: System and policy administrator
targets.needs: Manage access, policy, and audit history.
targets.pain: Operators cannot immediately tell where an asset is or who uses it, while employees cannot easily check availability or their return date.

scenarios.text: An employee finds an available projector for an event, requests it, and checks it out with a QR code after approval.
An operator records an asset whose location differs during a scheduled inventory audit.
System and policy administrator reviews policy and exception handling status.
System and policy administrator reviews policy and exception handling status.

prd.northStar: Share of active assets with a verified record

kpis.name: Asset-ledger accuracy
kpis.method: Matched assets ÷ audited assets in regular inventory counts

kpis.name: Checkout handling time
kpis.method: Measure time from request creation to approval or rejection

inScope: Asset registration, QR identity, status, location, and assignment
Checkout requests, approval, pickup, return, and overdue follow-up
Inventory audits, condition correction, and maintenance context
Asset, checkout, overdue, and audit operations reporting
Asset registration and QR identity validation and duplicate prevention
nonGoals: Mobile device management or remote device control
assumptions: Employee and organization data are available through company SSO.
Each asset can receive a unique asset number or QR label.
risks: Inaccurate initial records may reduce operator trust.
Damaged QR labels or missing scan devices may disrupt field operations.
openQuestions: Which asset classes can be checked out without approval?
How will overdue alerts and recovery be operated?
constraints: Assignment and location changes must create audit logs.
Employees can view only their own loans and assets marked as discoverable.
Permission, policy, and state changes require audit logs.
